meta.title: Domingo González de Lena Díaz · personal archive
meta.description: Public archive of Domingo González de Lena Díaz: countryside and game warden, mushroom grower, author of an exhibition project on Asturian Palaeolithic art (8 caves, 25 pieces), writings and Asturian song scores.

hero.title: Domingo González de Lena Díaz
hero.subtitle: Countryside and game warden, mushroom grower, reproducer of Asturian Palaeolithic art.
hero.lead: Five facets in one archive: the exhibition project of 25 stone pieces from Asturian caves, the mycology courses, the short writings, the music and the biography. Edited by his son Pelayo González de Lena Rodríguez.

manifesto.title: Manifesto
manifesto.lead: The project turns Domingo González de Lena Díaz's sculptural corpus into a public-access infrastructure to the Palaeolithic engraving of the Nalón.
manifesto.verbs: Three guiding verbs: reproduce, symbolically preserve, democratise.
manifesto.warning: Reproductions work as tools of interpretation, education and tactile accessibility. The original heritage stays in the caves. The trilinear hind is treated as a proposed visual emblem, with the caution the bibliography demands.

cierva.title: The trilinear hind
cierva.lead: Three lines linking origin, community and future.
cierva.body: The term describes a recurring graphic treatment in the Franco-Cantabrian region: three parallel strokes that delimit the silhouette of the cervid. The project takes the motif as a curatorial and communicative emblem, and records the open scientific debate in its catalogue and bibliography.

biografia.subtitulo: Domingo was born in {lugar} on 15 June {anho}.
biografia.intro: Hunter from the Valgrande valley, field naturalist and author of the exhibition project on Asturian Palaeolithic art. The timeline is built from the personal archive: the CV closed in February 2009, the mycology course posters, letters and writings.
biografia.para_saber_mas_intro: The other sections of the archive expand each facet with pieces, prices and contact routes.

gallery.title: Gallery
gallery.subtitle: Sculptural corpus photographically documented by Amanda C. Blanco in April 2026.

personalGallery.title: Personal archive gallery
personalGallery.subtitle: A selection of photographs Domingo has kept over the years: the trip to Naples, the Pravia season and older items from the family archive.
personalGallery.creditLine: Personal archive of Domingo González de Lena Díaz. Reproduction only with the author's permission.
personalGallery.corpusNote: Looking for the sculptural corpus gallery photographed by Amanda C. Blanco?

catalog.title: Corpus numbers
catalog.subtitle: Live figures of the catalogue and project KPIs.

map.title: Map of the Oviedo Nalón
map.subtitle: Sites within the Oviedo concejo and the five Asturian UNESCO caves.

research.title: Recent publications
research.subtitle: Open research on Cantabrian Palaeolithic art, the trilinear hind and the Nalón sites. Data via OpenAlex.
research.error: OpenAlex did not respond. Try again in a few minutes.
research.methodNote: Queries combine title, abstract and concepts. Results may include works not exclusive to the Nalón. Use as a panoramic view, not as a closed bibliography.

resources.title: Resources
resources.subtitle: Links, datasets, regulations and bibliography for research, press, teaching and cultural administration.

eventos.title: Events
eventos.subtitle: Exhibitions, talks and activities by Domingo González de Lena. This page updates with every confirmation.
eventos.contact: Get in touch about a venue
eventos.emptyTitle: Domingo is preparing his next exhibition
eventos.emptyBody: Confirmed dates will appear here. If you would like to host a selection from the corpus at your venue, write to him directly.
eventos.calendarHint: The «Subscribe to calendar» link opens an iCalendar (.ics) file compatible with Google Calendar, Apple Calendar and Outlook.

solicitud.title: Request an exhibition or loan
solicitud.lead: If you represent a gallery, a library, a school or a town hall, this form opens an email to Domingo with the data prefilled. There is also a WhatsApp option.
solicitud.nota: This form sends nothing to a server: it opens your email client or WhatsApp with the message ready to send.

commissions.title: Commissions
commissions.lead: Domingo carves the stone by hand at his workshop in Lena. Below are the available pieces, real prices, honest lead times and direct contact. No middlemen.
commissions.preciosNota: List written by the author. Prices in euros, VAT not included. Small stones: 5 €.

destacados.title: Pieces available now
destacados.lead: Fifteen selected pieces from the catalogue, with photo and price. To reserve one, a WhatsApp message is enough.
destacados.fichasNota: The descriptions are being written together with Domingo. For now we show reference, photo and price.

casos.title: What an order is for
casos.lead: Eight situations in which people usually call.

regalo.title: Personal gift
regalo.body: Anniversaries, weddings, retirements. A stone piece with a Palaeolithic motif, signed by Domingo.

interior.title: Interior decoration
interior.body: Living rooms, offices, hallways. Small pieces (15 to 50 €) fit on a shelf.

exterior.title: Garden and outdoors
exterior.body: Medium and large pieces for gardens, patios and terraces. They handle the Asturian climate.

publico.title: Public space and monument
publico.body: Squares, parks and roundabouts. Domingo has produced monumental pieces on municipal commission.

coleccion.title: Private collection
coleccion.body: For collectors of cave art, ethnography or contemporary Asturian sculpture.

personalizado.title: Custom commission
personalizado.body: If you have a specific motif in mind (an animal, an engraving from a particular cave), Domingo carves it to order.

institucional.title: Institutional work
institucional.body: Universities, museums, town councils and cultural centres. Domingo invoices as a self-employed artist.

cuevas.title: Souvenir of Asturian caves
cuevas.body: Faithful reproductions of engravings from Pindal, La Lluera, Tito Bustillo, La Peña de Candamo and others.

plazos.title: Lead times
plazos.lead: Domingo works by hand at his workshop. Lead times depend on the weather, the available stone and the complexity of the motif. For a specific date, just ask.

pagos.title: Accepted payment methods
pagos.lead: Domingo invoices as self-employed. The methods available today:

transferencia.title: Bank transfer
transferencia.body: Account details sent privately when the order is confirmed.

efectivo.title: Cash at the workshop
efectivo.body: If you collect the piece in Lena, you can pay in cash.

paypal.title: PayPal
paypal.body: Available with an added cost for the platform fee.

tarjeta.title: Card in person
tarjeta.body: Mobile card reader for in-person payments at the workshop or arranged delivery.

fraccionado.title: Split payment
fraccionado.body: Personal arrangement if the piece is over 200 €. Half on order, half on delivery.

envios.title: Shipping costs
envios.lead: Indicative rates. Pieces travel in a custom box, which adds 3 to 5 € to the shipping cost.

ue.coste: 25 to 60 € depending on weight and country

contacto.title: Talk to Domingo
contacto.lead: The fastest channel is WhatsApp. Email is also available.
contacto.horario: Usual hours: Monday to Saturday, 10:00 to 20:00.

devoluciones.title: Returns policy
devoluciones.body: If the piece arrives broken in transit, Domingo replaces it or refunds the amount without argument. If the piece does not convince you on receipt, you have 14 calendar days to return it in perfect condition; return shipping is paid by the buyer. Custom pieces cannot be returned except for manufacturing defects.

form.name: Name and surname
form.consent: I agree that the Trilineal team contacts me by email to reply to this request.
form.ok: Request sent. You will receive a reply within five working days.
form.error: There was a problem sending the form. Please try again or write to domingodelena@gmail.com.

about.title: About the project
about.committeeBody: Seven members in formation, one per area: Cantabrian Palaeolithic art, Nalón archaeology, conservation, museography, accessibility, feminist heritage studies and European cultural management.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lead: For press, institutions, research and collaboration.
